title: Blockcontrol - Interoperable Blockchain Data Custody Protocol
author: Rakic, Dean
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The Blockcontrol IBDCP (Interoperable Blockchain Data Custody Protocol) is a novel protocol among existing Blockchain protocols (decentralised and distributed) and traditional centralised data structures utilised for data transfer or transactional data processing. The IBDCP is a data custody protocol for transactional data processing, which plays a key role in solving various data verification and storage problems. It ensures that people have responsibility and control over their data, especially regarding storage, access, transparency, security, traceability, and auditability. By resolving the interoperability issue at the zero level, it addresses the issue of interoperability in data transaction processing across all domains of data usage by providing a solution for uninterrupted transfer and smooth interaction, eliminating the challenges and obstacles that arise when diverse entities or systems must collaborate. While retaining complete control and responsibility over their data sets, secure data exchange effectively addresses the challenges of blind trust in data sources and thus contributes to preventing malicious content in data transaction processing.

It ensures that people have responsibility and control over their data, especially regarding storage, access, transparency, security, traceability, and auditability. By resolving the interoperability issue at the zero level, it addresses the issue of interoperability in data transaction processing across all domains of data usage by providing a solution for uninterrupted transfer and smooth interaction, eliminating the challenges and obstacles that arise when diverse entities or systems must collaborate. While retaining complete control and responsibility over their data sets, secure data exchange effectively addresses the challenges of blind trust in data sources and thus contributes to preventing malicious content in data transaction processing.
